_meta.keyKnowledge: We are learning to notice and respond to sounds around us and to words we know.

_source.w: We are learning to notice and respond to sounds around us and to words we know.
y: Listening to sounds is how we start to understand language.
When we respond to words, it shows we are making connections between sounds and meaning.
Noticing sounds in our environment keeps us safe and helps us learn.
eg: We turn our head when we hear someone call our name.
We look towards the door when we hear a knock.
We get excited when we hear the word for something we like, such as 'play'.
We respond to the sound of rain or music during a sensory activity.
We look at a sign or symbol when someone reads the word on it.
We stop what we are doing when we hear a familiar instruction.
